face_analytics_consent_message: This function includes biometric technology that extracts and matches facial feature data for identification purposes. Depending on the purpose of use and the location where the product is installed, certain legal obligations may arise. Please review the following carefully before activating this function. 1. Prior Consent Requirement: The identification, storage, or use of a specific individual through facial recognition may require the explicit prior consent of the data subject under applicable laws and regulations, including but not limited to the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Korean Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), and the U.S. Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA). 2. Protection of Non-Registered Individuals: For individuals whose explicit consent has not been obtained, this product supports functionality that does not permanently store facial feature data. Such data is processed only through real-time analysis and is immediately deleted or converted into non-identifiable ephemeral metadata. (The customer is responsible for maintaining such settings in compliance with applicable laws.) 3. Responsibility of the Operator: Responsibility for compliance with applicable laws and regulations — including the selection of installation locations, operational methods, providing notice to data subjects, and obtaining any required consent — rests with the user (customer) operating this function. Suprema acts solely as a technology provider and does not intervene in or control the specific manner in which the customer deploys or operates the system. 4. BIPA-Related Notice: If this function is used in jurisdictions where biometric information protection laws are strictly applied (such as the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act – BIPA), failure to obtain written consent prior to collection may result in direct civil liability and statutory damages. 5. EU AI Act and Regulatory Compliance Notice: The user acknowledges that the deployment and use of biometric identification technologies may constitute high-risk AI system usage under applicable regulations, including the EU Artificial Intelligence Act, depending on the specific context of use. The user is responsible for ensuring that the deployment and operation of this function comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and regulatory requirements.
face_analytics_consent_checkbox: I confirm that I have fully understood the above information and agree to activate the facial recognition function under my responsibility to comply with all applicable laws and regulations.
face_analytics_consent_title: Notice and Consent for Activation of Vionyx Facial Recognition Function

min_face_size_guide_content: Face Recognition Minimum Size This feature helps you understand the minimum face size required for accurate facial recognition in the camera view. How it works: • Click "Visualize" to show face detection areas on the camera view • The displayed icons represent the minimum size a face must be for reliable recognition • Faces smaller than this size may not be detected consistently Guidelines: • Larger minimum size: More accurate detection, fewer false positives • Smaller minimum size: Detects distant faces, but may increase false detections • Position the minimum size icons to match your monitoring requirements Best Practice: Adjust the minimum face size based on the distance between the camera and the area you want to monitor for optimal recognition accuracy.
min_face_size_help_message: What is Min Face Size? The minimum pixel size required for face recognition. Recommended Settings: • 50px: Standard distance detection (recommended) • 30px: Long distance detection • 70px: Close range high-precision detection Notes: • Smaller values may increase false positives • Larger values may reduce recognition rate • Adjust according to camera resolution
